heading: Issuance of restraining orders and injunctions; limitation; public policy
label: 101 Issuance of restraining orders and injunctions; limitation; public policy
text: No court of the United States, as defined in this chapter, shall have jurisdiction to issue any restraining order or temporary or permanent injunction in a case involving or growing out of a labor dispute, except in a strict conformity with the provisions of this chapter; nor shall any such restraining order or temporary or permanent injunction be issued contrary to the public policy declared in this chapter.
notes: Short Title, , which enacted this chapter, is popularly known as the “Norris-LaGuardia Act”.Act Mar. 23, 1932, ch. 9047 Stat. 70
